_comment: Ground truth for pbs-agent-rag-v0.2. Each hop has a regex that defines the set of KB chunks supporting that fact (mega whole-document chunks > 20000 chars are excluded by verify_rag.py). 'question' is the task's main question, used for the naive single-query top-8 multi-hop check.

tasks.question: A process is terminated by the SIGABRT signal. What numeric exit status does bash report in $? for it?

hops.name: rule-128-plus-n
hops.fact: a command terminated by signal N has exit status 128+N

hops.name: sigabrt-is-6
hops.fact: SIGABRT is signal number 6

tasks.question: A foreground script is killed by SIGHUP when its terminal closes. What numeric exit status does the parent shell see in $??

hops.name: rule-128-plus-n
hops.fact: a command terminated by signal N has exit status 128+N

hops.name: sighup-is-1
hops.fact: SIGHUP is signal number 1

tasks.question: A command is killed by SIGALRM. What numeric exit status does bash assign to it?

hops.name: rule-128-plus-n
hops.fact: a command terminated by signal N has exit status 128+N

hops.name: sigalrm-is-14
hops.fact: SIGALRM is signal number 14

tasks.question: After `set -- a b c` with IFS left at its default value, what exact string does "$*" (in double quotes) expand to?

hops.name: star-joins-first-ifs-char
hops.fact: in double quotes $* joins the positional parameters with the first character of IFS
hops.regex: separated by the first character|joined.{0,60}first character

hops.name: ifs-default-space-tab-newline
hops.fact: the default IFS is space, tab, newline

tasks.question: After a two-command pipeline runs, $? only reflects the last command. What exact parameter expansion yields the exit status of the FIRST command of the most recently executed pipeline?

hops.name: pipeline-status-is-last
hops.fact: the exit status of a pipeline is the exit status of its last command

hops.name: pipestatus-array
hops.fact: PIPESTATUS is an array of the exit statuses of the most recent foreground pipeline

tasks.question: One signal can never be caught or ignored with trap. Using the POSIX numeric signal numbering, what is that signal's NUMBER?

hops.name: sigkill-untrappable
hops.fact: SIGKILL cannot be trapped/caught/ignored

hops.name: sigkill-is-9
hops.fact: SIGKILL is signal number 9

tasks.question: A script starts a background job with &. Using the special parameter that expands to that job's PID, what exact two-word command waits for it and sets $? to its exit status?

hops.name: bang-is-bg-pid
hops.fact: $! expands to the PID of the job most recently placed into the background

hops.name: wait-builtin
hops.fact: wait blocks until the given child process exits and returns its exit status

tasks.question: A process is forcibly killed with SIGKILL. What numeric exit status does bash report for it in $??

hops.name: rule-128-plus-n
hops.fact: a command terminated by signal N has exit status 128+N

hops.name: sigkill-is-9
hops.fact: SIGKILL is signal number 9

tasks.question: A long-running command is terminated with the kill builtin called with no signal option (just the PID). What numeric exit status does bash report for that command?

hops.name: kill-default-sigterm
hops.fact: kill sends SIGTERM when no sigspec/signum is given

hops.name: sigterm-is-15
hops.fact: SIGTERM is signal number 15

hops.name: rule-128-plus-n
hops.fact: a command terminated by signal N has exit status 128+N

tasks.question: A user presses Ctrl-\ to kill the foreground job. What numeric exit status does the shell report for that job?

hops.name: ctrl-backslash-sigquit
hops.fact: Ctrl-\ sends SIGQUIT to the foreground job

hops.name: sigquit-is-3
hops.fact: SIGQUIT is signal number 3

hops.name: rule-128-plus-n
hops.fact: a command terminated by signal N has exit status 128+N

tasks.question: A user presses Ctrl-C to interrupt the foreground job. What numeric exit status does the shell report for that job?

hops.name: ctrl-c-sigint
hops.fact: Ctrl-C sends SIGINT to the foreground job

hops.name: sigint-is-2
hops.fact: SIGINT is signal number 2

hops.name: rule-128-plus-n
hops.fact: a command terminated by signal N has exit status 128+N

tasks.question: With `set -o pipefail` enabled, what is the value of $? after running the pipeline `( exit 3 ) | true`?

hops.name: pipefail-semantics
hops.fact: with pipefail, a pipeline's status is the last command to exit with a non-zero status (rightmost failure)

hops.name: exit-n-returns-n
hops.fact: exit n makes the shell return status n

hops.name: true-returns-0
hops.fact: true does nothing and returns status 0

tasks.question: How do I pass all positional parameters to a command as one single argument (a single word, separated by spaces, IFS untouched)?

near_miss.fact: "$@" is the much more commonly recommended form, but it expands to SEPARATE words, one per parameter

hops.name: star-joins-first-ifs-char
hops.fact: in double quotes $* expands to a single word joined by the first character of IFS
hops.regex: separated by the first character|joined.{0,60}first character

hops.name: ifs-default-space-tab-newline
hops.fact: the default IFS starts with a space, so the join character is a space

tasks.question: The variable flags='-a -b -c' must be passed to a command as three separate arguments (POSIX-compatible script, no arrays). When you write $flags on the command line, should the expansion be double-quoted or left unquoted?
tasks.answer_regex: leave[^.]{0,40}unquoted|double quotes? may be safely omitted

near_miss.fact: the dominant guidance ('quote variables', 'use "$@"', 'use arrays for argument lists') is the near-miss: quoting $flags passes ONE argument, and arrays are excluded by the task
near_miss.regex: (?i)quote variables|should have used an array|arrays should be used|[Dd]ouble quote to prevent|[Ww]hen in doubt, quote

hops.name: unquoted-expansion-word-splits
hops.fact: the shell scans the results of expansions that did NOT occur within double quotes for word splitting
hops.regex: did not occur within double quotes

hops.name: quotes-omitted-for-splitting
hops.fact: double quotes may be deliberately omitted / expansions left unquoted when splitting is wanted
hops.regex: (double quotes? may be safely omitted)|(leave (the outside |expansions? )?unquoted)
